**Egidio Gennari** (20 April 1876 – 8 April 1942) was an Italian communist politician who was among the founders of [Italian Communist Party](/wiki/Italian_Communist_Party "Italian Communist Party"). He served at the Italian Parliament between 1924 and 1926 when he was forced to leave Italy due to the repression of the [Fascist government](/wiki/Fascist_Italy_(1922%E2%80%931943) "Fascist Italy (1922–1943)"). He died in exile in the [Soviet Union](/wiki/Soviet_Union "Soviet Union").

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Egidio_Gennari&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Gennari was born in [Albano Laziale](/wiki/Albano_Laziale "Albano Laziale") on 20 April 1876.[[1]](#cite_note-dbi-1) He was a teacher by profession.[[2]](#cite_note-jrid-2) He joined the [Italian Socialist Party](/wiki/Italian_Socialist_Party "Italian Socialist Party") in 1897 and headed its left wing.[[2]](#cite_note-jrid-2) He adopted an [internationalist approach](/wiki/Internationalism_(politics) "Internationalism (politics)") during [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I").[[2]](#cite_note-jrid-2) He served as the political secretary of the Italian Socialist Party in 1920 replacing [Nicola Bombacci](/wiki/Nicola_Bombacci "Nicola Bombacci") in the post.[[3]](#cite_note-3) He was a vice chairman of Presidium of the [Third World Congress of the Comintern](/wiki/3rd_World_Congress_of_the_Comintern "3rd World Congress of the Comintern").[[2]](#cite_note-jrid-2) He also participated in several plenums of the [Communist International](/wiki/Communist_International "Communist International") or Comintern in the 1920s.[[4]](#cite_note-vkah-4) His [pseudonym](/wiki/Pseudonym "Pseudonym") in these meetings was *Maggi*.[[4]](#cite_note-vkah-4)

In 1921 Gennari cofounded the Italian Communist Party.[[5]](#cite_note-trec-5) In 1923 the party leader [Amadeo Bordiga](/wiki/Amadeo_Bordiga "Amadeo Bordiga") was arrested, and the Comintern [Executive Committee](/wiki/Executive_Committee_of_the_Communist_International "Executive Committee of the Communist International") assigned a group of party members to lead the party, including Gennari, [Mauro Scoccimarro](/wiki/Mauro_Scoccimarro "Mauro Scoccimarro"), [Palmiro Togliatti](/wiki/Palmiro_Togliatti "Palmiro Togliatti"), [Angelo Tasca](/wiki/Angelo_Tasca "Angelo Tasca") and [Umberto Terracini](/wiki/Umberto_Terracini "Umberto Terracini").[[6]](#cite_note-6) In the party Gennari was part of the faction led by [Antonio Gramsci](/wiki/Antonio_Gramsci "Antonio Gramsci").[[7]](#cite_note-7) Gennari was elected as a deputy in 1924.[[5]](#cite_note-trec-5)

In 1926 he settled in the Soviet Union when the Fascist repression intensified.[[1]](#cite_note-dbi-1) Gennari represented the Italian Communist Party in an international conference organized against war and fascism held in Paris on 23 and 24 November 1935 shortly after the [invasion of Abyssinia](/wiki/Second_Italo-Ethiopian_War "Second Italo-Ethiopian War") by the Italian Fascist forces.[[8]](#cite_note-8) He died after a serious illness in [Gorky, Russia](/wiki/Nizhny_Novgorod "Nizhny Novgorod"), on 8 April 1942.[[1]](#cite_note-dbi-1)
